revision.text:
[[ja:天豊足柄姫命神社]] {{Expand Japanese|天豊足柄姫命神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Amenotoyotarashikarahimenomikoto Shrine | native_name = 天豊足柄姫命神社 | image = 天豊足柄姫命神社.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Amenotoyotarashikarahimenomikoto Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.900674|132.078841}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }} {{nihongo|'''Amenotoyotarashikarahimenomikoto Shrine'''|天豊足柄姫命神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Iwami Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}} * {{ill|Q135026601|ltq=Q135026601|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11587179|ltq=Q11587179|qid=Q11587179|1=List of Shikinaisha in Iwami Province}} ** Series ordinal: 20 ** P155: {{ill|Taimasan Shrine|lt=Taimasan Shrine|ja|大麻山神社|qid=Q30925653|1=Taimasan Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Q135041127|ltq=Q135041127|qid=Q135041127|1=Oomatsuriamaihakatohikono Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q432055|ltq=Q432055|qid=Q432055|1=Hamada, Shimane}} * {{ill|Naka District, Shimane|lt=Naka district|en|Naka District, Shimane|ja|那賀郡 (島根県)|zh|那賀郡 (島根縣)|qid=Q6960105|1=Naka District, Shimane}} * {{ill|Q1053709|ltq=Q1053709|qid=Q1053709|1=Iwami Province}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|34.900674|132.078841}} == image (P18) == * 天豊足柄姫命神社.jpg == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 182977 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q90139226|ltq=Q90139226|qid=Q90139226|1=Ken-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Shinto Directive|lt=Shinto Directive|en|Shinto Directive|fa|بخشنامه شینتو|fr|Directive shinto|id|Pengarahan Shinto|ja|神道指令|zh|神道指令|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * あめのとよたらしからひめのみことじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 8280005004184 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県浜田市殿町七七番地', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:天豊足柄姫命神社]] {{wikidata link|Q24863642}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine | Name = Ameno-Toyotarashikarahimenomikoto Shrine | Image = [[File:Ameno-Toyotarashikarahimenomikoto Shrine.jpg|250px]]
Torii | Location = {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|qid=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Hamada City|Q432055|qid=Q432055|1=Hamada, Shimane}} Tonochō 77 | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Ameno-Toyotarashikarahimenomikoto|Q24863814|qid=Q24863814|1=Ameno-Toyotarashikarahimenomikoto}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyoukehimenomikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (minor) · Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Ameno-Toyotarashi
Karahime Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Amen o-Toyotarashikarahimenomikoto Shrine''' (あめのとよたらしからひめのみことじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|qid=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}, {{ill|lt=Hamada City|Q432055|qid=Q432055|1=Hamada, Shimane}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined deities === The main enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Ameno-Toyotarashikarahimenomikoto|Q24863814|qid=Q24863814|1=Ameno-Toyotarashikarahimenomikoto}} (あめのとよたらしからひめのみこと), with {{ill|lt=Toyoukehime|Q2983724|qid=Q2983724|lt=Toyoukehimenomikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}} enshrined together. The subsidiary shrine within the precincts, Kameyama Shrine, enshrines successive lords of the Hamada Domain. Although the deeds of the main deity are not clearly known, she is revered as a goddess who taught the ways of clothing and food. The former *History of Shimane Prefecture* argues regarding Ameno-Toyotarashikarahimenomikoto: “The stone deity is merely a temporary vessel of a single generation; the true enshrined deity is Ameno-Toyotarashikarahimenomikoto.” === History === The circumstances of the shrine’s founding are unknown. It is presumed to have flourished as a Shikinaisha, but from the medieval period onward it declined amid continued warfare, remaining in ruin for several hundred years. In the Meiji period, following the abolition of the domains and establishment of prefectures, a new shrine building was erected. In Meiji 6 (1873) it became a prefectural shrine, and in July of Meiji 8 (1875) it became a village shrine. In Bunmei 18 (1486), the shrine buildings were reconstructed. In Genna 6 (1620), Furuta Shigeharu entered Hamada from Matsusaka in Ise Province, constructed Hamada Castle, and enshrined the deity within the castle grounds. In Meiji 7 (1874), both the main hall and the worship hall were constructed. {{Quotation| The stone deity is a god enshrining Ameno-Toyotarashikarahimenomikoto of Iwami. According to its tradition, the notion that the deity turned into stone has no basis and cannot be believed. It is surmised that the deity opened up Iwami Province and bestowed merit upon the people, who, admiring and revering this virtue, built a shrine hall and enshrined the deity. Considering that it is listed as a Shikinaisha, one can know how large the shrine hall was at that time and how solemn and prosperous the worship was. From the medieval period onward, calamities and unrest continued, worship could not be carried out, and it fell into ruin for several hundred years. In the Meiji era, domains were abolished and prefectures established, and Nobuhiro came to govern this region. He reported to the Imperial Court his reverence for the kami, and as this place had an association with the stone deity, restoration thereafter began. Here, a new shrine hall was built and worship resumed; it was deliberated to make it a prefectural shrine and to encourage the prefectural people to venerate it perpetually. This was inscribed on stone in order to record this matter. January, Meiji 7 | Governor of Hamada Prefecture, Senior Sixth Rank, Sato Nobuhiro, composed | Explanation of the monument inscription}} === References === {{No footnotes|date=June 2017|section=1}} * Research Society for Shikinaisha, ed., *Shikinaisha Survey Report, Vol. 21, San’indō 4*, Kogakkan University Press, 1983. * *Jingi Zensho, Vol. 5*, Shibunkaku, 1971. ※ Includes Fujii Muneo, “Study on the Locations of Shikinaisha in Iwami Province.” * Shimane Prefectural Department of Education, Shimane Prefectural History Compilation Office, ed., *History of Shimane Prefecture*, Shimane Prefecture, 1922. * Iwami Region Unpublished Materials Publication Society, ed., *Kakushōkyō Iwami Yaemugura*, Iwami Region Unpublished Materials Publication Society, 1999. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Tourist sites of Shimane Prefecture|Q11476446|qid=Q11476446|1=Tourist sites of Shimane Prefecture}} * {{ill|lt=Ameno-Toyotarashikarahimenomikoto|Q24863814|qid=Q24863814|1=Ameno-Toyotarashikarahimenomikoto}} === External links === == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ame-no-Toyotarashikara-hime-no-mikoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ame-no-Toyotarashikara-hime-no-mikoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ame-no-Toyotarashikara-hime-no-mikoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Iwami Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:浜田市の建築物]] [[Category:浜田市の歴史]] [[Category:石見国の式内社]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:浜田藩]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hamada, Shimane]]
